summary: Iteration 24 - Hierarchical Topology Map Feature Validation. Both backend and frontend testing completed successfully. Backend topology inference engine returns correct hierarchical structure with 9 nodes, 8 edges, 4 layers, and 88% health score. Frontend renders SVG topology map with proper hierarchical layout, animated connections, device badges, and health gauge.

backend_topology_api: Response has required fields: nodes, edges, layers, health, client_id, client_name
Topology has 4 hierarchical layers: WAN, Firewall/Router, Switch Core, Accesso/Server/Mgmt
Health score calculated correctly: score=88, reachability=100, latency_score=50, port_health=100
Device types classified correctly: 1 firewall, 6 switches, 1 iLO
Edge types correct: 1 WAN, 2 trunk, 4 access, 1 mgmt
Node roles assigned: gateway, core_switch, access_switch, management
Invalid client ID returns empty topology
frontend_topology_map: View mode toggle visible with Lista and Mappa buttons
Click Mappa switches to map view
Network map container visible with data-testid='network-map'
Client topology panel visible with data-testid='topo-client-{id}'
Health score 88% displayed in header and as gauge in SVG
SVG element visible with hierarchical layout
Legend shows 4 connection types: WAN, Trunk, Accesso, Management
Layer labels visible: WAN, FIREWALL/ROUTER, SWITCH CORE/DISTRIBUZIONE, ACCESSO/SERVER/MGMT
Toggle back to Lista view works correctly

critical_code_review_comments: Topology inference engine correctly classifies devices by type (firewall, switch, iLO, etc.)
Hierarchical layout algorithm properly assigns layers based on device roles
Health score calculation uses weighted formula: 50% reachability + 25% latency + 25% port health
Frontend NetworkMap component uses SVG for rendering with proper data-testid attributes
Connection lines show different styles for WAN (cyan), Trunk (indigo), Access (gray), Management (amber dashed)
Animated connection pulses indicate active links
HealthGauge component renders circular progress indicator
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - used existing 86BIT_Office client with 8 devices
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hierarchical topology map feature fully validated. Backend endpoint GET /api/network/topology/{client_id} returns graph structure with nodes, edges, layers, and health score. Frontend renders SVG map with 4 hierarchical layers: WAN -> Firewall -> Core Switches -> Access/Server/Mgmt. All 8 devices reachable (green status). Health score 88%. Toggle between Lista/Mappa views works correctly.
